perturbation.original_text: In this Agreement the following expressions shall have the following meanings: (a) 'Purpose' shall mean any discussions , negotiations and development work undertaken between or within the parties concerning or in connection with Switchdig ital and the Applicant entering into a carriage agreement for carriage of the Applicant’s radio service on the DAB multiplex operated by Switchdigital ;
perturbation.changed_text: In this Agreement the following expressions shall have the following meanings: (a) 'Objective' shall mean any discussions , negotiations and development work undertaken between or within the parties concerning or in connection with Switchdig ital and the Applicant entering into a carriage agreement for carriage of the Applicant’s radio service on the DAB multiplex operated by Switchdigital ;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Purpose' is changed to 'Objective' here. Later in the contract, 'Purpose' is used. This creates confusion as to whether 'Objective' is synonymous with 'Purpose'. The original definition for 'Purpose' is now absent, creating ambiguity. This misinterpretation may lead to disputes regarding the scope of permissible activities under the agreement, because now it's not clear if 'Objective' is the same as 'Purpose'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, (i) not to make any commercial use thereof (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third party other than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any in formation, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including, without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one of the exceptions in clause 1; (b) not to copy reproduce or reduce to writing any part thereof except as may be reasonably necessary for the Purpose and that any copies reproductions or reductions to writing so made shall be the property of the disclosing party;
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Purpose' with 'Objective' in the definition section. The contradicted text uses the original term 'Purpose'. Since 'Purpose' is no longer defined, and the term in the contradicted text isn't updated, there is ambiguity and a direct contradiction. The Misaligned Terminology creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (b) 'Confidential Information' shall mean all information or data disclosed (whether in writing, orally or b y any other means) to one party by the other party or by a third party on behalf of the other party and shall include but not be limited to (A) any information ascertainable by the inspection or analysis of samples, (B) the information described in the Sch edule 1 hereto as having been disclosed prior to the date hereof and (C) any information relating to that party's business, operations, prices, processes, plans, intentions, product information, know - how, design rights, trade secrets, software, market oppo rtunities, customers and business affairs, but shall exclude any part of such disclosed information or data which: (i) is or becomes in the public domain in any way without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; or
perturbation.changed_text: (b) 'Secret Information' shall mean all information or data disclosed (whether in writing, orally or b y any other means) to one party by the other party or by a third party on behalf of the other party and shall include but not be limited to (A) any information ascertainable by the inspection or analysis of samples, (B) the information described in the Sch edule 1 hereto as having been disclosed prior to the date hereof and (C) any information relating to that party's business, operations, prices, processes, plans, intentions, product information, know - how, design rights, trade secrets, software, market oppo rtunities, customers and business affairs, but shall exclude any part of such disclosed information or data which: (i) is or becomes in the public domain in any way without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; or
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Secret Information' in the definition but retaining 'Confidential Information' elsewhere introduces inconsistency. Later clauses refer to handling 'Confidential Information', creating ambiguity about whether those clauses apply to what is now defined as 'Secret Information'. This could lead to disputes about the scope of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of the mutual exchange and disclosure of Confidential Information, each party undertakes in relation to the other party's Confidential Information: (a) to maintain the same in confidence and to use it only for the Purpose and for no other purpose and in particular, but without prejudice to the generality of the foregoing, (i) not to make any commercial use thereof (ii) not to use the same for the benefit of itself or of any third party other than pursuant to a further agreement with the other party and (iii) not to use the same for the purpose of guiding or conducting a search of any in formation, materials or sources, whether or not available to the public, for any purpose whatsoever, including, without limitation, for the purpose of demonstrating that any information falls within one of the exceptions in clause 1;
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information,' which is then referenced in the 'Contradicted Text.' The changed text redefines 'Confidential Information' as 'Secret Information.' Because the contradicted text explicitly refers to 'Confidential Information,' changing the definition creates a contradiction since the contradicted text no longer aligns with the defined term, and creates ambiguity about what is covered by the clause.

perturbation.original_text: Each party reserves all rights in its Confidential Information and no rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licence is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or other industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by either party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party reserves all rights in its Sensitive Information and no rights or obligations other than those expressly recited herein are granted or to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licence is hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, invention, discovery, copyright or other industrial property right now or in the future held, made, obtained or licensable by either party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Information' but not defining 'Sensitive Information' and continuing to use 'Confidential Information' elsewhere creates confusion. This raises questions about which type of information is protected by the agreement and whether 'Sensitive Information' and 'Confidential Information' have different scopes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall: (a) within one month of completion of the Purpose or receipt of a written request from the other party, return to the other party all documents and mater ials (and all copies thereof) containing the other party's Confidential Information and certify in writing to the other party that it has complied with the requirements of this sub -clause; and (b) notwithstanding completion of the Purpose or return of document s and materials as aforesaid, continue to be bound by the undertakings set out in clause 2.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is likely defined elsewhere in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Information' but does not define 'Sensitive Information'. The 'Contradicted Text' refers to 'Confidential Information'. Because 'Sensitive Information' is undefined, and 'Confidential Information' is still used, it is unclear which information is protected, and whether obligations related to 'Confidential Information' apply to 'Sensitive Information'. This is an in-text contradiction according to the provided definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement is personal t o the parties and shall not be assigned or otherwise transferred in whole or in part by either party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract is personal t o the parties and shall not be assigned or otherwise transferred in whole or in part by either party without the prior written consent of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Interchanging 'Agreement' with 'Contract'. The text defines 'Agreement', which is now inconsistently referred to as 'Contract'. It's now not clear, which is being reffered to, the 'Contract' or the 'Agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement the following expressions shall have the following meanings: (a) 'Purpose' shall mean any discussions , negotiations and development work undertaken between or within the parties concerning or in connection with Switchdig ital and the Applicant entering into a carriage agreement for carriage of the Applicant’s radio service on the DAB multiplex operated by Switchdigital ; (b) 'Confidential Information' shall mean all information or data disclosed (whether in writing, orally or b y any other means) to one party by the other party or by a third party on behalf of the other party and shall include but not be limited to (A) any information ascertainable by the inspection or analysis of samples, (B) the information described in the Sch edule 1 hereto as having been disclosed prior to the date hereof and (C) any information relating to that party's business, operations, prices, processes, plans, intentions, product information, know - how, design rights, trade secrets, software, market oppo rtunities, customers and business affairs, but shall exclude any part of such disclosed information or data which: (i) is or becomes in the public domain in any way without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; or - 2 -
perturbation.justification: The perturbation explanation states that changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' creates inconsistency because the text defines 'Agreement'. The 'Contradicted Text' section refers to "this Agreement" repeatedly. By changing the term in the Changed Text to 'Contract', it creates a strong in-text contradiction because the definitions and obligations outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' section now ambiguously refer to either 'Agreement' (as defined) or 'Contract' (undefined), causing direct confusion and potential unenforceability, which aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement ca nnot be changed except by written agreement between the parties. The interpretation construction and effect of this Agreement shall be governed and construed in all respects in accordance with English Law and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdictio n of the courts of England and Wales.
perturbation.changed_text: This document constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement ca nnot be changed except by written agreement between the parties. The interpretation construction and effect of this Agreement shall be governed and construed in all respects in accordance with English Law and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdictio n of the courts of England and Wales.
perturbation.explanation: Here we changed 'Agreement' to 'document'. As 'Agreement' has a defined meaning, changing it to 'document' creates uncertainty as it's no longer clear if all previous references to the agreement apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This document constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement ca nnot be changed except by written agreement between the parties. The interpretation construction and effect of this Agreement shall be governed and construed in all respects in accordance with English Law and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdictio n of the courts of England and Wales.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'document' introduces ambiguity. While the contradicted text refers to 'Agreement', the changed text uses 'document'. This creates an in-text contradiction because it's no longer clear if all previous references to the agreement apply, thus conflicting with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.
